title: 'As he approached the sands of the Arabian-Persian Gulf, Trump fell prey to a common phenomenon in these lands: mirages'
subhead: The American president's disappointments and contradictory statements on the conflict in Iran obscure the key question of how to end the war, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: EU Energy Commissioner Dan Jorgensen: 'Capping gas prices is a possibility'
ICC member states divided over prosecutor Karim Khan's fate
In Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, 'no one ventures outside anymore apart from rescue teams'
subhead: Like 42 other rescue workers since the war began on March 2, 16-year-old Joude Souleiman was killed by an Israeli drone strike in Nabatieh, the second-largest Shiite city in southern Lebanon, where only a few hundred people out of 75,000 residents remain.

title: French prime minister pushes on as 2025 public deficit beats forecasts
subhead: France's public deficit stood at 5.1% of GDP in 2025, beating the target of 5.4%, according to data released Friday. The positive surprise boosts the credibility of the 5% target in 2026, despite the oil shock.

title: Cybercrime, the blind spot in museum security
subhead: Dozens of online ticketing systems in France's cultural sector were shut down following a large-scale cyberattack targeting Vivaticket, the leading operator in the field, on March 2. French institutions are still reviewing their defense systems.

title: 'As soon as a peace agreement or ceasefire is reached in Ukraine, the Russians will be able to rebuild,' warns Sweden's chief of military operations
subhead: Vice Admiral Ewa Skoog Haslum tells Le Monde she is concerned about the adaptation and 'remodeling' of Russian forces who 'have learned a great deal from the war in Ukraine.'

Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president expressed concern about the consequences of the war in the Middle East on the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow. Pressured by both the Americans and the Russians to give up the Donbas, he defended the region's strategic importance and warned Europeans of the urgent need to lift the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan approved in 2025.

title: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
subhead: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

title: The US and Israel are fighting different wars against the same foe
subhead: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu share many objectives. However, their respective public opinions and electoral interests may ultimately highlight significant differences between the two leaders.

title: Courts finally recognize dangers of social media
'In Paris, the ideal of urban transformation clearly resonated with voters'
Pierre Buhler, former diplomat: We are witnessing 'a worrying crack in the European edifice'
